Ultra-fast fashion giant Shein is planning to acquire former sustainable fashion wunderkind Everlane, in a deal valuing the latter brand at $100 million. Critics call the reported sale hypocrisy, others say matching fast fashion's speed and volume is the only way forward for #sustainability. If ever there was a metaphor for the state of sustainable fashion in 2026, this is it.
With so many other sustainable fashion brands closing their doors entirely, it poses an existential question: is slower growth, built on craft and traditional values, even possible in today’s market? Or does this deal sound the alarm for #sustainablefashion as we know it? Sustainability editor @BellaWebb_ and contributing editor @SophieBenson weigh in at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
It’s more nuanced. I believe the most interesting and creative work is quieter, and more elusive.
If attention becomes the objective, creativity becomes performance, an albatross of our current interests. I view the difference as long-term equity building and more interesting to commit to.
I think that is why brands like Rick Owens or Bottega Veneta feel distinct, while everyone else is seemingly chasing readability and validation.
The real 🐘 in the room, and a conversation we also should be having, is content fatigue and audience apathy towards any campaign that dares to try something different.
There is credibility to consider why consumers (especially young demographics) often are resorting online to performative outrage, to score social points with brands that provoke. That’s what gets rewarded.

posts.caption:
Two days after the official drop date, the hype behind the Audemars Piguet x Swatch Royal Pop collection remains strong. Today, Vogue Business was on the ground in London to see the continued consumer pull towards the £335 pocket watches.
In the morning, queues wrapped around the block at both Oxford Street and Covent Garden flagship stores with people waiting to get their hands on the limited-edition drop. Only 30 watches were delivered to Oxford Street today; they sold out instantly on a first-come, first-served basis. Many were still hopeful later on in the afternoon, but were met with temporarily closed storefronts and a police presence following the initial morning crowds.
The majority of interested buyers were resellers, some buying on behalf of their bosses, with groups of friends, others with parents, or independently. One anonymous bystander said, “I went to White City on Saturday morning, and people didn’t really know how to act. It’s excessive. There were kids in buggies, grown people and elderly people.”
Video and reporting: @_aureliarachel

posts.caption:
The women’s world number one @ArynaSabalenka understands that tennis can be opera: full of triumph and despair, grace and slapstick, sin and atonement. The 28-year-old has been at the top of her sport for the last year and a half, with a deep bench of brand associations—including with Gucci, Nike, and Audemars Piguet—to show for it. But since her earliest days on the tour, she’s been a fiery on-court presence, known for her outbursts (she’s a world-class racket-smasher) and tense exchanges with officials. (She attributes much of this to being “very much a Taurus”).
Time and training have helped her to temper that fire—but it also has its uses. “This is part of my personality that can drive me crazy, but it can also drive me into that real fight mode and help me play with passion,” Sabalenka says. “It’s two sides of the medal.”
For Vogue’s May 2026 digital cover, @robertjhaskell meets Aryna Sabalenka in Rome to talk winning, losing, grieving, learning, and never letting up.

posts.caption:
There has long been more than a whiff of a double standard when it comes to expressed emotion in women’s tennis, and anachronistic notions about what is “ladylike” persist in fan forums and even among some commentators. “@ArynaSabalenka’s learned how emotions are information. These days she can take that information and process it better," says her fitness trainer, Jason Stacy. "It’s the progression from fighter to warrior. She was that young fighter, running off adrenaline, surviving the moment. Warriors are calmer. They can zoom out and then zoom back in and refocus on the right thing.”
For Vogue’s May 2026 digital cover, @robertjhaskell meets Aryna Sabalenka in Rome to talk winning, losing, grieving, learning, and never letting up.

posts.caption: There’s a new sound coming out of Norway that’s like nothing you’ve ever heard before. It’s produced by twenty-two year old rappers HC (@hchiace) and Subaru (@zubaro111), who perform together as Bygdetrapen, and who have created a sub genre called rural trap. The duo’s focus is on the hyper local and personal. Their subject is life in their small town in the Telemark region, where skiing originated. (Think snowmobiles, cars, inside jokes, friends.) Most radically, they sing in their local dialect, which has roots in Old Norse. “Our stuff is kind of only made to be understood by a couple hundred people who know the area we’re from,” said Subaru, yet their music has a growing appeal to masses of fans (like @the_lbp) who can’t understand what  Bygdetrapen is saying, but cannot stop listening. Tune in to the new sound by tapping the link in bio.

posts.caption:
@maudeapatow is ready now. Fresh off her return in the highly anticipated third season of #Euphoria, Apatow is stepping into a new chapter with the kind of clarity and control that only comes from growing up in real time—on-screen, off-screen, and alongside co-stars who have gone on to navigate intergalactic levels of fame and careers of their own. "Just thinking about us being little kids and having no idea what was in store to now… It's insane," she says.
In our May cover story, Apatow opens up about the roles that shaped her, the relationships that fulfilled her, the theater work that grounded her, and the rituals—travel, matcha, and all—that keep her moving forward. Read our full interview with the actress and director at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Many actors would feel burned out toward the end of a press tour — but not @perfect_angelgirl, who is refreshingly excited about having to promote her work.
“I just felt like such a professional actor, honestly. I was like, ’wow, look at me in New York City doing press for my show. I’m booked up and I’m doing all the press,’” she says. “I felt like it was just so deeply validating as an actor.”
The 28-year-old stars as Faye in “Euphoria,” a character that was introduced in the second season and elevated in the third one, airing now. Season Three Faye is the biggest role of Cherry’s yet, having previously worked in the adult entertainment industry, and she’s relishing every moment of the experience.

@chanelofficial is enjoying high-single-digit growth year to date after returning to growth last year as it reorganized its teams and bolstered its manufacturing capacity under new creative director @matthieu_blazy.⁠
⁠
The French fashion house said full-year revenues rose 3 percent at reported exchange rates to $19.3 billion, representing an increase of 1.8 percent in comparable terms. This marked a turnaround from the previous year, when revenues were down 4.3 percent at comparable rates amid a sharp slowdown in spending in mainland China.⁠

The @swatch x @audemarspiguet Royal Pop collaboration is already showing signs of volatility on the secondary market, with resale prices dropping sharply from their initial peaks, while still holding above retail.⁠
⁠
Early transactions surged in the immediate aftermath of Saturday’s release.⁠
⁠
On Sunday, the multicolor-on-white Huit Blanc model sold for as much as 2,022 euros before falling to current listings around $1,295. A similar pattern played out with the blue Savonnette Lan Ba colorway, which reached 4,310 euros at its peak the same day and is now listed closer to 1,431 euros.⁠
⁠

For her latest appearance on the red carpet, @rileykeough was drawn to something softer.⁠
⁠
For her the choice was simple. “It was just one of the most beautiful things I’ve ever seen in my life,” she says of the wisp-thin and delicate skirt combo.⁠
⁠
It was an atypical red carpet selection. Instead of a voluminous gown, this was almost impossibly light. But the choice fit neatly into the mood currently surrounding @chanelofficial — a house Keough has long orbited — since @matthieu_blazy has taken it in a new direction.⁠
⁠
